name: Serial Proxy
description: > [!IMPORTANT] > This component is EXPERIMENTAL. The API (both Python configuration and C++ interfaces) > may change at any time without following the normal breaking changes policy. Use at your own risk. > Once the API is considered stable, this warning will be removed.

config_entries.label: Name
config_entries.description: A human-readable name for this serial port, used to identify it to API clients.

config_entries.label: UART ID
config_entries.description: The ID of the [UART](https://esphome.io/components/uart/) component this proxy is attached to.

config_entries.label: Dtr Pin
config_entries.description: GPIO pin to use as the DTR (Data Terminal Ready) modem control output. The state of this pin may be controlled by the API client at runtime.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: Port Type
config_entries.description: The electrical type of the serial port. One of:

options.label: Standard TTL logic-level UART (3.3V or 5V). Typically used for direct connections to most microcontrollers.
RS-232 standard serial port (typically ±12V levels via an appropriate line-driver IC). Typically used for PC-style serial connections, often with a DB9-type connector.
RS-485 differential serial bus. Typically used for industrial/multi-drop serial networks.

config_entries.label: Rts Pin
config_entries.description: GPIO pin to use as the RTS (Request to Send) modem control output. The state of this pin may be controlled by the API client at runtime.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID to use for code generation.
